rulings.Case Title: STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY VS. PAUL MICHAEL SHEPHERD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Or Notice Of Settlement And Enter Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2019, Line 1, PLAINTIFF STATE FARM MUTUAL AUTOMOBILE INSURANCE COMPANY's Motion To Set Aside Dismissal And Or Notice Of Settlement And Enter Judgment. Off calendar. The motion reflects that after entering into the Stipulation for Entry of Judgment, defendant Paul Michael Shepherd moved to Pacifica, California, where defendant sent him the Notice of Default on February 22, 2019. However, plaintiff served the instant motion on defendant at his former address in Daly City. =(302/EPS)

rulings.Case Title: DENTAL FORCE, INC. ET AL VS. BEMDAD BEHNOOD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION Submitted By Plaintiffs Dental Force, Inc. And Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, Pc For Summary Adjudication As To The Third Cause Of Action In Their Complaint And Summary Adjudication As To The Fifth, Eighth, Thirteenth, And Fourteenth Affirmative Defenses In The Answer Of Defendant Bemdad Behnood To Plaintiffs Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Motion For Summary Adjudication (Redacted)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, September 19, 2019, Line 3, PLAINTIFFS DENTAL FORCE, INC., EVANGELISTA DENTAL GROUP SAN FRANCISCO 450 SUTTER's MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION As To The Third Cause Of Action In Their Complaint And Summary Adjudication As To The Fifth, Eighth, Thirteenth, And Fourteenth Affirmative Defenses In The Answer Of Defendant Bemdad Behnood To Plaintiffs Complaint. Plaintiffs Dental Force Inc. and Evangelista Dental Group San Francisco 450 Sutter, PC's motion for summary adjudication is denied in part and granted in part. [The full tentative ruling was emailed to the parties.] Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for the plaintiffs is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EPS)
